function fetchRSSFeed(url) { fetch("https://morseinstitute.assabetinteractive.com/calendar/upcoming-events.exml?filter-categories%5b%5d=adults&filter-branches%5b%5d=morse-institute-library&filter-branches%5b%5d=virtual") .then(response => response.text()) .then(data => { const parser = new DOMParser(); const xmlDoc = parser.parseFromString(data, "text/xml"); const items = xmlDoc.querySelectorAll("item"); const eventsList = document.getElementById("eventsinfo"); const eventsList2 = document.getElementById("eventsinfo2"); const eventsList3 = document.getElementById("eventsinfo3"); if (items.length > 0) { const firstEvent = items[0]; // Get the first event const title = firstEvent.querySelector("title").textContent; const description = firstEvent.querySelector("description").textContent; const date = firstEvent.querySelector("date1").textContent; const startTime = firstEvent.querySelector("time").textContent; const endTime = firstEvent.querySelector("endtime").textContent; const location = firstEvent.querySelector("location").textContent; const link = firstEvent.querySelector("link").textContent; const parts = date.split("/"); const shuffledDate = parts[1] + "/" + parts[2] + "/" + parts[0]; const divTitle = document.createElement("div"); const divTime = document.createElement("div"); const divDate = document.createElement("div"); const divLocation = document.createElement("div"); divTitle.className = "title"; divTime.className = "time"; divDate.className = "date"; divLocation.className = "location"; const anchorTitle = document.createElement('a'); anchorTitle.setAttribute('href', link); anchorTitle.innerText =`${title}`; divTitle.appendChild(anchorTitle); const anchorTime = document.createElement('a'); anchorTime.setAttribute('href', link); anchorTime.innerText = `${startTime} - ${endTime}`; divTime.appendChild(anchorTime); const anchorDate = document.createElement('a'); anchorDate.setAttribute('href', link); anchorDate.innerText = `${shuffledDate}`; divDate.appendChild(anchorDate); const anchorLocation = document.createElement('a'); anchorLocation.setAttribute('href', link); anchorLocation.innerText = `${location}`; divLocation.appendChild(anchorLocation); eventsList.appendChild(divTitle); eventsList.appendChild(divTime); eventsList.appendChild(divDate); eventsList.appendChild(divLocation); } else { // Handle case when no events are found const divTitle = document.createElement("div"); divTitle.innerText = "No upcoming events found"; eventsList.appendChild(divTitle); } }) .catch(error => { console.error("Error fetching RSS feed:", error); }); } rssUrl = "https://morseinstitute.assabetinteractive.com/calendar/upcoming-events.exml?filter-categories%5b%5d=adults&filter-branches%5b%5d=morse-institute-library&filter-branches%5b%5d=virtual"; fetchRSSFeed(rssUrl);
function fetchRSSFeed(url) { fetch("https://morseinstitute.assabetinteractive.com/calendar/upcoming-events.exml?filter-categories%5b%5d=teens&filter-branches%5b%5d=morse-institute-library&filter-branches%5b%5d=virtual") .then(response => response.text()) .then(data => { const parser = new DOMParser(); const xmlDoc = parser.parseFromString(data, "text/xml"); const items = xmlDoc.querySelectorAll("item"); const eventsList = document.getElementById("eventsinfo2"); if (items.length > 0) { const firstEvent = items[1]; // Get the second event const title = firstEvent.querySelector("title").textContent; const description = firstEvent.querySelector("description").textContent; const date = firstEvent.querySelector("date1").textContent; const startTime = firstEvent.querySelector("time").textContent; const endTime = firstEvent.querySelector("endtime").textContent; const location = firstEvent.querySelector("location").textContent; const link = firstEvent.querySelector("link").textContent; const parts = date.split("/"); const shuffledDate = parts[1] + "/" + parts[2] + "/" + parts[0]; const divTitle = document.createElement("div"); const divTime = document.createElement("div"); const divDate = document.createElement("div"); const divLocation = document.createElement("div"); divTitle.className = "title"; divTime.className = "time"; divDate.className = "date"; divLocation.className = "location"; const anchorTitle = document.createElement('a'); anchorTitle.setAttribute('href', link); anchorTitle.innerText =`${title}`; divTitle.appendChild(anchorTitle); const anchorTime = document.createElement('a'); anchorTime.setAttribute('href', link); anchorTime.innerText = `${startTime} - ${endTime}`; divTime.appendChild(anchorTime); const anchorDate = document.createElement('a'); anchorDate.setAttribute('href', link); anchorDate.innerText = `${shuffledDate}`; divDate.appendChild(anchorDate); const anchorLocation = document.createElement('a'); anchorLocation.setAttribute('href', link); anchorLocation.innerText = `${location}`; divLocation.appendChild(anchorLocation); eventsList.appendChild(divTitle); eventsList.appendChild(divTime); eventsList.appendChild(divDate); eventsList.appendChild(divLocation); } else { // Handle case when no events are found const divTitle = document.createElement("div"); divTitle.innerText = "No upcoming events found"; eventsList.appendChild(divTitle); } }) .catch(error => { console.error("Error fetching RSS feed:", error); }); } rssUrl = "https://morseinstitute.assabetinteractive.com/calendar/upcoming-events.exml?filter-categories%5b%5d=teens&filter-branches%5b%5d=morse-institute-library&filter-branches%5b%5d=virtual"; fetchRSSFeed(rssUrl);
function fetchRSSFeed(url) { fetch("https://morseinstitute.assabetinteractive.com/calendar/upcoming-events.exml?filter-categories%5b%5d=children&filter-branches%5b%5d=morse-institute-library&filter-branches%5b%5d=virtual") .then(response => response.text()) .then(data => { const parser = new DOMParser(); const xmlDoc = parser.parseFromString(data, "text/xml"); const items = xmlDoc.querySelectorAll("item"); const eventsList = document.getElementById("eventsinfo3"); if (items.length > 0) { const firstEvent = items[0]; // Get the first event const title = firstEvent.querySelector("title").textContent; const description = firstEvent.querySelector("description").textContent; const date = firstEvent.querySelector("date1").textContent; const startTime = firstEvent.querySelector("time").textContent; const endTime = firstEvent.querySelector("endtime").textContent; const location = firstEvent.querySelector("location").textContent; const link = firstEvent.querySelector("link").textContent; const parts = date.split("/"); const shuffledDate = parts[1] + "/" + parts[2] + "/" + parts[0]; const divTitle = document.createElement("div"); const divTime = document.createElement("div"); const divDate = document.createElement("div"); const divLocation = document.createElement("div"); divTitle.className = "title"; divTime.className = "time"; divDate.className = "date"; divLocation.className = "location"; const anchorTitle = document.createElement('a'); anchorTitle.setAttribute('href', link); anchorTitle.innerText =`${title}`; divTitle.appendChild(anchorTitle); const anchorTime = document.createElement('a'); anchorTime.setAttribute('href', link); anchorTime.innerText = `${startTime} - ${endTime}`; divTime.appendChild(anchorTime); const anchorDate = document.createElement('a'); anchorDate.setAttribute('href', link); anchorDate.innerText = `${shuffledDate}`; divDate.appendChild(anchorDate); const anchorLocation = document.createElement('a'); anchorLocation.setAttribute('href', link); anchorLocation.innerText = `${location}`; divLocation.appendChild(anchorLocation); eventsList.appendChild(divTitle); eventsList.appendChild(divTime); eventsList.appendChild(divDate); eventsList.appendChild(divLocation); } else { // Handle case when no events are found const divTitle = document.createElement("div"); divTitle.innerText = "No upcoming events found"; eventsList.appendChild(divTitle); } }) .catch(error => { console.error("Error fetching RSS feed:", error); }); } rssUrl = "https://morseinstitute.assabetinteractive.com/calendar/upcoming-events.exml?filter-categories%5b%5d=children&filter-branches%5b%5d=morse-institute-library&filter-branches%5b%5d=virtual"; fetchRSSFeed(rssUrl);
LIBRARY HOURS Mon-Thurs:
Rooms & Technology
#center-content-grid{ display: grid; grid-template-columns: repeat(2, 1fr); grid-template-rows: repeat(2, 1fr); row-gap: 20px; column-gap: 15px; height: 100%; } .court-button { background-color: #003e72; color: white; border-radius: 5px 5px 5px 5px; width: 100%; height:auto; min-height: 5vh; border-width: 0px; transition: 0.35s; } .court-button:hover { filter: brightness(130%); } .center-content-panes{ display: grid; grid-template-columns: repeat(3, 1fr); grid-template-rows: repeat(4, auto); height: 100%; width: 100%; border-radius: 10px 10px 10px 10px; border: 5px solid #003e72; background-color: #003e72; transition: 0.35s ease; } .center-content-panes:hover{ border:5px solid #ffa500; transition: 0.35s ease; } .center-content-panes-text{ background-color: #256295; grid-row: 2/5; grid-column: 1/3; width: 100%; height: 100%; border-radius: 0px 0px 0px 5px; z-index: 2; color: white; font-size: 20px; font-family: "Crimson Pro", serif; text-align: center; padding-left: 3%; padding-right: 3%; padding-bottom: 3%; padding-top: 10%; } .center-content-panes-text p{ font-family: "Crimson Pro", serif; } .center-content-panes-title{ background-color: #003e72; grid-row: 1/2; grid-column: 1/3; width:100%; height:100%; font-size: 20px; text-align: center; color: white; border-radius: 3px 0px 0px 0px; padding: 3%; } .center-content-panes-title h2{ font-family: "Archivo", sans-serif; margin-top: 0px; margin-bottom: 0px; } .center-content-blank{ border-radius: 5% 5% 5% 5%; grid-row: 1/4; grid-column: 2/3; width: 100%; height: 100%; color: black; background-image: url("https://libapps.s3.amazonaws.com/accounts/202073/images/woman-6784555_960_720.jpg"); background-position: center; background-repeat: no-repeat; background-size:cover; } #img-text{ float:right; background-color: #003e72; border-radius: 0% 10% 0% 10%; width: 33%; height: 60%; color: white; text-align: center; font-size: 30px; font-family: "Archivo", sans-serif; opacity: 0.95; } #s-la-widget-12362 .btn-default{ border: 3px solid #256295; transition: 0.35s ease; width: 90%; height: 50px; font-size: 14px; } .sr-btn{ border: 2px solid #003e72; transition: 0.35s ease; } .sr-btn:hover { border: 2px solid #ffa500 transition: 0.35s ease; } #s-la-widget-12362 .btn-default:hover{ border: 3px solid #ffa500; background-color: white; transition: 0.35s ease; } #s-la-widget-12362{ background-color: transparent; font-family: "Archivo", sans-serif;; padding-top: 30%; } #s-la-widget-12631 .btn-default{ border: 3px solid #256295; transition: 0.35s ease; width: 90%; height: 50px; font-size: 17px; } #s-la-widget-12631 .btn-default:hover{ border: 3px solid #ffa500; background-color: white; transition: 0.35s ease; } #s-la-widget-12631{ background-color: transparent; font-family: "Archivo", sans-serif;; padding-top: 10%; padding-bottom: 10%; } #helpcenter { border: 3px solid #256295; transition: 0.35s ease; width: 90%; height: 50px; font-size: 18px; font-family: "Archivo", sans-serif; } #helpcenter:hover{ border: 3px solid #ffa500; background-color: white; transition: 0.35s ease; } .center-content-panes-image{ grid-column:3/4; grid-row: 1/5; background-color: white; border-radius: 0px 5px 5px 0px; } .center-content-anchor:hover{ text-decoration: none; } #anchor-libby { grid-row: 1/2; grid-column: 1/2; } #anchor-overdrive { grid-row: 2/3; grid-column: 1/2; } #anchor-kanopy { grid-row: 3/4; grid-column: 1/2; } #Kanopy-fix{ background-color: black; border-radius: 0px 8px 8px 0px; } #img-text-header{ padding-top: 7%; } @media screen and (max-width: 1200px){ #center-content-grid{ grid-template-columns: 1fr; grid-template-rows: repeat(2, 1fr); } .center-content-blank{ display: none; } .center-content-panes{ grid-column: 1/2; grid-row: 1/3; width: 100%; } } @media screen and (max-width: 768px) { #center-content-grid{ grid-template-columns: 1fr; grid-template-rows: repeat(2, 1fr); } .center-content-blank{ display: none; } .center-content-panes{ grid-column: 1/2; grid-row: 1/3; width: 100%; } } Read with Libby
Read ebooks and listen to audiobooks from our library. Download the Libby app on your phone or tablet.
Looking for Overdrive?
Read ebooks and listen to audiobooks on Overdrive.
Kanopy offers more than 30,000 films, including critically acclaimed movies, inspiring documentaries, award-winning foreign films, and more.
Need a quiet place to attend a court meeting? Visit our Access to Justice page to find out to use the Morse Institute Library to attend court remotely.

pass_page.policy_text:
To limit museum types being displayed, check the corresponding categories:

MORSE INSTITUTE LIBRARY
To select a museum, click “Details/Reserve”, then select which Pass to reserve.
{"@context": "http://schema.org","@type": "CivicStructure","address": "568 Main Street, Hudson, MA 01749","logo": "https://s3.amazonaws.com/ai-assets/museum-logos/ma-american-heritage-museum_150_md.jpg","telephone": "978-562-9182","name": "American Heritage Museum","url": "","description": "The American Heritage Museum, located in Hudson, MA, is dedicated to military history. Exhibits examine conflicts from the Revolutionary War through 9/11 and the &ldquo;War on Terror.&rdquo; A good portion of the museum is focused on the sizable Littlefield collection, which includes many rare items from the U.S. and other countries. Among other artifacts, visitors to the museum will encounter aircraft, tanks, guns, and automobiles, some dating from the beginning of the 20th century."} American Heritage Museum 568 Main Street, Hudson, MA 01749978-562-9182https://www.americanheritagemuseum.org/[Details/Reserve]
American Heritage Museum The American Heritage Museum, located in Hudson, MA, is dedicated to military history. Exhibits examine conflicts from the Revolutionary War through 9/11 and the “War on Terror.” A good portion of the museum is focused on the sizable Littlefield collection, which includes many rare items from the U.S. and other countries. Among other artifacts, visitors to the museum will encounter aircraft, tanks, guns, and automobiles, some dating from the beginning of the 20th century.
Categories: Family, History, Military
Pass Type: Circulating Pass (must be picked up and returned to the branch)
Pass Benefits Type of pass: Returnable, must be picked up from the library the day before or the day of your reservation date.
Pricing: Reduced admission for up to 6 people at 50% off per person (children under 3 always free).
Additional instructions:-Present pass upon arrival to receive discount.-Pass is valid for $20 off a Family Membership or $10 off an Individual or Participating Membership purchase by the user.
THIS PASS MUST BE RETURNED TO THE MORSE INSTITUTE LIBRARY BY 9:30AM THE DAY AFTER YOUR RESERVATION. OVERDUE FINES ARE $3/DAY. Passes can be returned at the circulation desk or the outside book drops.
Reserve a Pass Reserve a Pass
{"@context": "http://schema.org","@type": "CivicStructure","address": "5 Water Street, Fall River, MA 02721","logo": "https://s3.amazonaws.com/ai-assets/museum-logos/ma-battleship-cove_285_md.png","telephone": "508-678-1100","name": "Battleship Cove","url": "","description": "Battleship Cove is a war memorial and historic military museum located in Fall River, Massachusetts. It is home to five National Historic Landmark ships, and other craft, including the USS Massachusetts, USS Joseph P. Kennedy Jr., USS Lionfish, Hiddensee, and two PT boats. Their collection of aircraft includes two Bell helicopters that saw combat in the Vietnam War&mdash;the Bell UH-1 &ldquo;Huey&rdquo; Iroquois and the Bell AH-1 Cobra&mdash;as well as two of the first drone unmanned aerial vehicles used by the military. In addition to exhibits focused on military history, the Maritime Museum presents the history of the RMS Titanic and the famous Fall River Line."} Battleship Cove 5 Water Street, Fall River, MA 02721508-678-1100https://www.battleshipcove.org/[Details/Reserve]
Battleship Cove Battleship Cove is a war memorial and historic military museum located in Fall River, Massachusetts. It is home to five National Historic Landmark ships, and other craft, including the USS Massachusetts, USS Joseph P. Kennedy Jr., USS Lionfish, Hiddensee, and two PT boats. Their collection of aircraft includes two Bell helicopters that saw combat in the Vietnam War—the Bell UH-1 “Huey” Iroquois and the Bell AH-1 Cobra—as well as two of the first drone unmanned aerial vehicles used by the military. In addition to exhibits focused on military history, the Maritime Museum presents the history of the RMS Titanic and the famous Fall River Line.
Categories: Children, Family, History, Military, Technology
Pricing: Reduced general admission for up to 2 adults and 3 children at $3 per person.
Additional instructions:-Present pass upon arrival to receive discount.
{"@context": "http://schema.org","@type": "CivicStructure","address": "308 Congress Street, Boston, MA 02210","logo": "https://s3.amazonaws.com/ai-assets/museum-logos/ma-boston-childrens-museum_3_md.jpg","telephone": "617-426-6500","name": "Boston Children&apos;s Museum","url": "","description": "Founded in 1913 by the Science Teachers&apos; Bureau, the Boston Children&apos;s Museum is the second oldest children&apos;s museum in the world and one of the few that maintain a collection. Designed for both children and families, the Museum exhibits focus on science, culture, environmental awareness, health & fitness, and the arts, emphasizing hands-on engagement and learning through experience, and employing play as a tool to spark the inherent creativity, curiosity, and imagination of children."} Boston Children's Museum 308 Congress Street, Boston, MA 02210617-426-6500http://www.bostonchildrensmuseum.org/[Details/Reserve]
Boston Children's Museum Founded in 1913 by the Science Teachers' Bureau, the Boston Children's Museum is the second oldest children's museum in the world and one of the few that maintain a collection. Designed for both children and families, the Museum exhibits focus on science, culture, environmental awareness, health & fitness, and the arts, emphasizing hands-on engagement and learning through experience, and employing play as a tool to spark the inherent creativity, curiosity, and imagination of children.
Categories: Children, Crafts, Family, Technology
Pass Type: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
Pass Benefits Type of pass: E-coupon, emailed upon reservation.
Pricing: Reduced admission for up to 4 people (adults or children) at 50% off per person.
Additional instructions:-Pre-registration is required. Visit the Boston Children’s Museum website to reserve.-When purchasing your timed ticket, expand the “Discount Programs” option and select “Half Price Library Timed Ticket.” The date of your library e-coupon must match the date of your visit.
This coupon is not your ticket.
You MUST make a reservation on the Museum's website.
Instructions:1. Visit the Boston Children's Museum website2. Select "Discount Programs"3. Select "Purchase Discount Program Tickets"4. Select "Half Price Library Timed Tickets"5. Select the correct date (much match coupon)6. Select the time slot7. Select the number of tickets (maximum of 4)8. Select "Add to Card"9. Select "Check Out" and complete your payment10. You will be sent tickets via email
The day of your visit, please bring both your coupon from the library and your tickets from the Museum.
Your coupon and tickets can be printed out or shown on a smartphone or other device.
